examples: They burned the house and his diaries

meanings.definition: shine intensely, as if with heat
examples: The coals were glowing in the dark

meanings.definition: cause a sharp or stinging pain or discomfort

examples: The sun burned off the fog
We combust coal and other fossil fuels

meanings.definition: feel strong emotion, especially anger or passion
examples: He was burning to try out his new skies
The car burns only Diesel oil

meanings.definition: burn, sear, or freeze (tissue) using a hot iron or electric current or a caustic agent
get a sunburn by overexposure to the sun

examples: burn off calories through vigorous exercise

meanings.definition: burn with heat, fire, or radiation
examples: The iron burnt a hole in my dress
she served us underdone bacon and burnt biscuits

meanings.definition: treated by heating to a high temperature but below the melting or fusing point
destroyed or badly damaged by fire
examples: a charred bit of burnt wood
a burned-over site in the forest
barricaded the street with burnt-out cars
